mode_selected_rationale: Pre-flight operator-directive discovery found 2 pending directives; highest-priority is DIRECTIVE-20260521-001-workspace-sync-disaster-postmortem (high). Per dispatcher rules, pending directives override Mode 1-4 selection. Directive scope: author PROP-NNN with workstreams A (workspace-sync safety) + B (clone hygiene) + C (integrity pruning), post-mortem, counter-arguments, and unpause checklist. Constraint: FUSE+API only, no clones.

modes_checked.mode_2_infrastructure: skipped (directive override); note: directive itself cites disk pressure as root cause

directive_handled.title_short: POSTMORTEM + STRUCTURAL FIX: workspace-sync catastrophic delete incident ea785c49

previous_followup.method: Spot-checked previous tinker run (tinker-2026-05-21T02-45) which documented the disaster as a P0. That run's recommendation aligns with the directive's now-issued scope; no separate previous-finding fix needed beyond authoring PROP-051.
previous_followup.open_issues_json_corruption: Same JSON-parse error at byte 45760 observed today as in the 02-45 run. NOT in scope for PROP-051 (see PROP-051 counter_arguments_addressed.q_f). Decider-side bug, awaiting separate fix.
previous_followup.prop_lifecycle_verification: skipped this run — directive scope is forward-looking PROP authorship, not verification of past PROPs. Resumes next run.

findings.title: Workspace-sync mass-delete disaster ea785c49 — PROP-051 authored per operator directive
findings.root_cause_one_line: Haiku LLM improvised a `git clone --no-checkout` + mtime-only diff path under disk pressure. That code path is NOT in workspace-sync.md (verified by grep). It was a model invention.
findings.action_taken_this_run: Authored PROP-051 with 7 patches across 3 workstreams + 6-counter-argument rebuttal + 14-step operator unpause checklist.
patches_in_prop_051: A4: Pre-push delete-sanity gate (50-delete cap, 10%-of-tree alt ceiling)
B2: EXIT/INT/TERM trap for clone cleanup

findings.title: monitor/decisions/open-issues.json unparseable (corruption persists across runs)

evidence.current_run_observation: Same JSON-parse error at byte 45760: closing array `]` followed by `{` opening new object outside the array. Affects ISS-2172 onward.
evidence.blast_radius: Any agent that JSON-parses open-issues.json (decider, integrity, tinker backlog-trend) cannot read it without manual repair. PROP-030 backlog-trend metrics SKIPPED this run for this reason.

findings.not_in_scope_for_this_run: PROP-051 is the directive's mandate; open-issues.json corruption is a separate decider-side bug. Recommended: separate decider-targeted PROP or operator-direct repair.
findings.recommendation: Operator should hand-repair the closing `]` to a `,` (or remove the stray `]` and rely on the existing comma after the previous object) to restore JSON validity. Then a future tinker run resumes PROP-030 backlog-trend computation.

findings.title: PROP-049's audit asserts --depth 50 already on workspace-sync.md line 25 of HEAD, but raw.githubusercontent.com shows un-flagged clone command
findings.evidence: GET https://raw.githubusercontent.com/funwithscience-org/dome-model-review/main/monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md at 2026-05-21T05:35Z → line 25 contains the un-flagged clone. PROP-049 status is design-pending-operator-review and its audit_results.S2 fuse_status='STALE' but applied_by claims 'operator-direct 2026-05-18 per DIRECTIVE-20260518-002 bug_summary'. Either the operator-direct edit was rolled back by the disaster-recovery force-reset (1d256277 predates the alleged 2026-05-18 edit by some duration; needs verification) or the assertion was inaccurate to start with.
findings.recommendation: When applying PROP-051 patch B1, this finding is moot — B1 explicitly adds --depth 50 with conflict_check note that PROP-049's B1 patch line is different (line 581 strand-rescue text). Coordinate the edits regardless. Tinker note: PROP-049 status field should be re-verified by next tinker run.

disk_state_at_end_of_run.tmp_ws_sync_2_status: not present (was cited as 491MB in directive evidence; appears to have been cleaned up during recovery)
disk_state_at_end_of_run.note: Disk is still tight (/ at 96%). PROP-051's Workstream A2 disk-pressure pre-flight would CURRENTLY abort the workspace-sync cycle at the 200MB-on-/tmp threshold (current /tmp has 396MB free on /; sessions has 2.0GB free). When the operator applies PROP-051 and unpauses, the first cycle may abort cleanly — that is the gate working, not a failure. Workstream C (integrity pruning) freeing ~25MB will not by itself rescue /; root-fs pressure is primarily system-image baseline per the Mode 2 doctrine.

diagnostic_constraints_honored.rationale: DIRECTIVE-20260521-001 scope_authority explicitly forbade full clones during this diagnostic run to avoid recreating the disk-pressure condition that caused the disaster.
